## Comparison: API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Documentation | Implementation |
|----------|--------|---------------|----------------|
| `/api/users` | GET | ✅ Documented | ✅ Implemented (UsersController.scala) |
| `/api/users` | POST | ✅ Documented | ✅ Implemented (UsersController.scala) |
| `/api/health` | GET | ✅ Documented | ✅ Implemented (Main.scala) |

### Summary

**All three endpoints are both documented AND implemented:**

1. **`GET /api/users`** — Listed in README, implemented in `UsersController.handle` which returns JSON array of users
2. **`POST /api/users`** — Listed in README, implemented in `UsersController.handle` which returns `{"status":"created"}` (Note: The TODO comment indicates the actual body parsing isn't complete, but the endpoint responds to POST requests)
3. **`GET /api/health`** — Listed in README, implemented in Main.scala as an anonymous function `_ => Response(200, "OK")`

There are no discrepancies between what's documented and what's implemented. The codebase is consistent in its API documentation.
